blogPosts.title: How to Maximize Your Social Security Benefits
blogPosts.author: Steve Chen
blogPosts.excerpt: Understanding when and how to claim Social Security can mean a difference of hundreds of thousands of dollars over your lifetime.

body.text: Social Security is the foundation of most Americans' retirement income. Yet most people leave money on the table by claiming too early or without a strategy.
You can claim as early as 62, but your benefit increases roughly 8% for each year you delay until age 70. For someone with a full retirement age benefit of $2,000/month:
Use the Boldin Social Security calculator to model different claiming strategies and see the lifetime impact.

seo.title: Maximize Social Security Benefits - Claiming Strategies | Boldin
seo.description: Learn when to claim Social Security to maximize your lifetime benefits. Free calculator and expert strategies.

blogPosts.title: How to Build a Retirement Budget That Actually Works
blogPosts.author: Steve Chen
blogPosts.excerpt: Most retirement budgets fail because they don't account for how spending changes over time. Here's a better approach.

body.text: The conventional wisdom says you need 80% of your pre-retirement income. But that one-size-fits-all rule ignores how dramatically spending shifts in retirement.
The Three Phases of Retirement Spending
Early retirement (65-75): Active years with travel and hobbies — spending is often higher than expected.
Middle retirement (75-85): Spending typically decreases as activity slows.
Late retirement (85+): Healthcare costs can cause spending to spike again.

seo.title: Retirement Budget Planning Guide | Boldin
seo.description: Build a retirement budget that accounts for changing spending patterns. Free tools and expert guidance.

blogPosts.title: Roth Conversions: The Complete Guide for 2026
blogPosts.author: Steve Chen
blogPosts.excerpt: A Roth conversion can save you hundreds of thousands in taxes over your lifetime — if you do it strategically.

body.text: Converting traditional IRA funds to a Roth IRA means paying taxes now to get tax-free growth and withdrawals later. The key is doing it in the right years.
The sweet spot is typically between retirement and age 73 (when RMDs begin), when your income — and therefore tax bracket — is lower than during your working years.

seo.title: Roth Conversion Guide 2026 - Strategy & Calculator | Boldin
seo.description: Complete guide to Roth IRA conversions. Learn when to convert, how much, and model the tax impact with our free calculator.
